title: Espeletia betancurii
taxonomy: Espeletia betancurii is classified within the family Asteraceae. This plant belongs to the genus Espeletia, which is native to the Andean region. The species Espeletia betancurii is a type of shrub that can be found in high-altitude environments. It is native to Colombia, specifically in the departments of Cundinamarca and Boyacá. Espeletia betancurii is often found growing alongside other species of plants that are adapted to similar high-altitude conditions.

intro: Espeletia betancurii, a member of the Asteraceae family, is a plant species native to the Andean region of South America, commonly known as the 'Queen of the Andes' due to its unique, rosette-like structure. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities that can help alleviate symptoms of fever, pain, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Espeletia betancurii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenes, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. In traditional medicine, the plant is often used to prepare teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various ailments, including fever, cough, and sore throat.
